name: Liberty Bank, National Association
description_short: OCC-chartered commercial bank headquartered in Irvine, California, serving businesses and individuals across Southern California and San Francisco Bay Area with full-service banking solutions.
description_long: Liberty Bank, N.A. is a $442 million OCC-chartered (federally regulated) commercial bank headquartered in Irvine, California. The bank was established to serve the financial needs of businesses, entrepreneurs, and individuals in Southern California markets spanning greater Orange and Los Angeles Counties, as well as the greater San Francisco Bay Area. The institution operates as a full-service commercial bank with a leadership team that includes experienced banking executives and board members with deep roots in California financial services. Liberty Bank offers personal banking services, small business banking solutions, and lending products designed for everyday customers and entrepreneurs. The bank provides mobile banking apps for both iOS and Android platforms, enabling customers to transfer money, pay bills, deposit checks, and request funds remotely. Customers can access online banking and cash management tools, with dedicated customer support available Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST at (800) 735-2465. The bank maintains a routing number (122243130) for transaction processing and offers branch locations throughout its service areas. Liberty Bank distinguishes itself through leadership continuity and specialized expertise. CEO and Chairman Don Griffith, along with President and COO Deborah Marsten, previously founded and led Grandpoint Capital and its subsidiary Grandpoint Bank, bringing operational stability and proven banking management. The board includes a retired FDIC executive with bank supervision experience, a retired bank executive with commercial credit expertise, and a real estate finance specialist with over 20 years of experience. This concentration of seasoned banking talent and regional focus differentiates the bank from larger national institutions. Liberty Bank serves a specific geographic and customer niche—mid-market businesses and individuals in California's major metropolitan areas. As a $442 million bank, it operates at a scale larger than community banks but smaller than major national banks, positioning it for customers seeking personalized service with institutional stability. The primary caveat is geographic limitation; the bank only serves Southern California and San Francisco Bay Area markets, making it inaccessible to customers outside these regions.
services: Small business banking accounts and services
Deposit products (implied through full-service bank designation)
pros: OCC-chartered federal bank with full banking license and regulatory oversight
Mobile banking apps available on both Apple and Google platforms for convenient account management
Experienced leadership team with 20+ years of banking history at previous institutions
Full-service commercial bank offering personal, business, and lending products
Online banking and cash management tools for business customers
Dedicated customer support line with published hours (M-F 9am-5pm PST)
FDIC-insured deposits through federal bank charter
cons: Geographic limitations: only serves Southern California and San Francisco Bay Area, not available nationwide
Relatively small asset base ($442 million) compared to major national banks, which may limit product sophistication or competitive rates
Website provides limited specific information about deposit rates, loan rates, fees, or detailed product specifications
No mention of mobile check deposit features, peer-to-peer payment capabilities, or advanced digital banking tools
Limited information about small business loan products, terms, or SBA lending availability despite marketing to business customers
best_for: California-based small business owners and entrepreneurs in Orange County, Los Angeles County, or San Francisco Bay Area seeking relationship banking
Individuals and families in Southern California or Bay Area markets who prefer local bank relationships over national chains
Commercial credit customers needing traditional lending products with experienced relationship managers
Business owners requiring cash management and online banking solutions for mid-sized operations
diagnosis: Liberty Bank is best for California-based small business owners, entrepreneurs, and individuals in the Orange County, Los Angeles, or San Francisco Bay Area who value personalized service from an established commercial bank with experienced management. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this bank only operates in specific California markets and is not accessible to customers outside Southern California or the Bay Area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: OCC-chartered commercial bank in Irvine, California serving Southern California and San Francisco Bay Area with full-service banking.
